description_long: This object consists of two mushrooms with vibrant red caps adorned with numerous white, irregularly shaped spots. The larger mushroom features a broad, slightly domed cap with a straight margin, while the smaller mushroom has a more spherical cap with similar white spotting. Both mushrooms have thick, cylindrical stems that are white at the base and transition to a darker, almost black color toward the cap. The surface of the caps appears smooth with a matte finish, and the stems have a slightly rougher texture. No gills are visible beneath the caps, and the mushrooms are depicted standing upright as if growing from the ground. The overall appearance is reminiscent of the iconic Amanita muscaria, often found in natural or park-like urban settings.
description: Two red-capped mushrooms with white spots and thick, white-to-black stems, standing upright. The caps are smooth and matte, with no visible gills underneath.
description_view_0: Front view showing both mushrooms, with the smaller one in front and the larger cap's white spots clearly visible.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the side of both mushrooms, showing the full height of the stems and the underside shading.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the larger mushroom's cap and stem, with the smaller mushroom mostly hidden.
description_view_3: Side-rear view displaying both mushrooms, with the smaller one behind the larger, and the cap's spotted pattern prominent.
